mnemonics: ARIMA Rhythms: ARIMA stands for Auto-Regressive Integrated Moving Average. Remember the order: A (Auto-Regressive) - R (Regressive part), I (Integrated for differencing), M (Moving Average) - A (Average part).
keyPoints: Stationarity is a key assumption for many time series models; data should have constant mean and variance over time.
The Autocorrelation Function (ACF) helps identify the order of Moving Average (MA) terms, while the Partial Autocorrelation Function (PACF) helps identify AutoRegressive (AR) terms.
Differencing (the 'I' in ARIMA) is a common technique to remove trend and achieve stationarity.
commonMistakes: Failing to check for and address non-stationarity before fitting an ARIMA model, leading to spurious results.
Overfitting the model by using unnecessarily high orders for p, d, or q without proper validation using metrics like AIC or BIC.
Misinterpreting the ACF and PACF plots, for example, confusing the cut-off for a slow decay, which leads to incorrectly identifying the model order.
